available: current PostgreSQL 18 and Patroni 4 primary documentation
implementation and adversarial evidence created in this run

background.experience: breaker isolation, transfer authorization, and black-start recovery
background.attention_bias: positive isolation before energizing an alternate path
background.counter_bias: rejects indicator lights as proof that a circuit is de-energized

readers.thesis: Database promotion becomes safe only after the old write path is positively fenced and independently acknowledged.
observations: The architecture repeatedly separates service availability from canonical authority.
The failover gate rejects promotion until fencing evidence exists.
uncertainties: The production fence mechanism and independent power path remain untested.

background.experience: reconstructing source lineages from divergent manuscripts
background.attention_bias: timeline ancestry, divergence points, and authoritative continuity
background.counter_bias: rejects the newest surviving copy as automatically canonical

readers.thesis: Failover authority depends on proving timeline ancestry and selecting the candidate whose WAL history preserves admitted continuity.
observations: PostgreSQL timelines encode branch ancestry after promotion.
The gate binds candidate timeline and archive continuity before authority transfer.
uncertainties: The production WAL rate and acceptable failover lag remain unmeasured.

agreements: Prevent two writable primaries before evaluating service availability.
Bind every promotion to timeline, lag, fence, and archive evidence.
disagreements: Reader A prioritizes physical isolation; Reader B prioritizes WAL lineage.
omissions_detected: The first design allowed promotion without proving the displaced primary rejected writes.
revisions_after_discussion: Require a negative write probe against the fenced former primary.

notes_added: Treat fencing acknowledgement as a prerequisite to database authority transfer.
Treat PostgreSQL timeline ancestry as the continuity graph for database identity.
notes_challenged: A DCS leader lock alone eliminates split-brain risk.

privacy.review: Synthetic technical abstractions and explicit corpus limits only.
